summary: Improved matching in src/melee/lb/lbcollision by bringing lbColl_80005C44 and lbColl_80009DD4 to 100%, restoring/improving lbColl_8000A78C and nearby collision/debug draw helpers, and fixing lbColl_80006E58 so collision distance is written through the explicit float output parameter. The decomp.dev bot reported +1016 matched bytes overall, with 2 new matches and 7 unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.why: Primary matching work: changed collision math/layout in lbColl_80005C44, lbColl_80006094, lbColl_800067F8, lbColl_80006E58, debug draw helpers, JObj matrix access, constants, and call sites.
Updated stale prototypes for matched helpers, replacing UNK_RET with void for lbColl_80008DA4 and lbColl_80009DD4.

what_changed: Matched lbColl_80005C44 and lbColl_80009DD4; decomp.dev reported lbColl_80005C44 moving from 94.77% to 100.00% and lbColl_80009DD4 from 99.11% to 100.00%.

Improved several still-unmatched lbcollision items: lbColl_8000A78C from 0.00% to 99.86%, lbColl_80006E58 from 64.88% to 81.05%, lbColl_80006094 from 65.34% to 78.74%, lbColl_800067F8 from 65.47% to 78.71%, plus .sdata and small lbColl_8000A584/lbColl_8000A95C gains.
Changed lbColl_80006E58's parameter order so float* arg8 moved after scl, arg10, and arg11, then updated call sites such as lbColl_80007BCC, lbColl_80007ECC, lbColl_8000805C, and lbColl_80008248.
Fixed lbColl_80006E58's collision-distance write: the diff changed writes from arg2->x to *arg8, matching the PR body's note that distance should be written through the explicit output parameter.

Added local static inline lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr with local assert strings "jobj.h" and "jobj", calls HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, and returns jobj->mtx; many direct HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr uses were replaced with this local helper.
Added GXColor sdata symbols lbColl_804D36C4 and lbColl_804D36C8 and restored lbColl_8000A78C, using those colors in lbColl_800096B4 debug draw output.

Removed the generic between helper and changed nearzero to load thresholds from existing lbColl constants through volatile const float pointers; replaced approximatelyZero calls with nearzero in several collision routines.

Reworked Vec3/local-stack layout and scalar temporaries across collision math, including d2_x/d2_y/d2_z in lbColl_80005C44, Vec3 sp11C/sp110/sp104 in lbColl_80006E58, by-value Vec3 copies, PAD_STACK changes, and reordered locals.
smart_moves: Used a file-local lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr inline instead of relying on the existing HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr symbol, preserving the assert text, setup call, and matrix access sequence needed for matching.

Treated the function signature of lbColl_80006E58 as both a matching lever and a semantic fix: moving the float* output parameter clarified call-site order and made the distance write go to arg0->coll_distance instead of corrupting a Vec3 field.

Loaded floating constants through volatile pointers to named data symbols such as lbColl_804D79F0, lbColl_804D79F4, lbColl_804D79F8, lbColl_804D7A00, and lbColl_804D7A10, avoiding compiler folding or wrong-width literal emission.
Scalarized selected Vec3 temporaries and used whole-struct Vec3 copies where the compiler's stack/register behavior needed nudging, rather than only changing algebraic expressions.
Inlined simple early-out tests and adjusted parentheses around dot-product/division expressions to force codegen closer to the target assembly.
Synchronized the public header once functions were matched, preventing stale UNK_RET prototypes from lingering for lbColl_80008DA4 and lbColl_80009DD4.
decomp_lessons: For lbcollision, small source-level choices around Vec3 copies, scalar temporaries, local declaration order, and PAD_STACK size have large effects on stack layout and FPR allocation.

When a helper function looks semantically equivalent but does not match, check whether the original code likely inlined an assert/setup sequence; recreating that inline locally can recover sdata and call order.
Use existing rodata/sdata symbols for magic floats when exact load behavior matters; volatile casts can stop the compiler from turning a load into an immediate or different constant path.
Do not ignore prototype/order mismatches: lbColl_80006E58's signature change both improved matching and exposed a real output-parameter bug.
Header cleanup is part of decomp completion. Once a function's return type and parameters are known, replace UNK_RET/anonymous prototypes with the matched signature.

The PR body explicitly records remaining known blockers; future work should continue from lbColl_80006094, lbColl_800067F8, lbColl_80006E58, lbColl_800077A0, and the shared by-value Vec3 temporary mismatch family.
naming_conventions: Function names follow the project address-style lbColl_800XXXXX convention, e.g. lbColl_80005C44, lbColl_80006E58, lbColl_80009DD4, and lbColl_8000A78C.
Data symbols use address-style lbColl_804Dxxxx or lbl_803Bxxxx names until better semantic names are known, e.g. lbColl_804D36C4, lbColl_804D36C8, lbColl_804D79F0, and lbl_803B9E20.
The local inline helper was named lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr, keeping the lbColl prefix while describing its HSD_JObj matrix access role.
Matched header prototypes use concrete return types and parameter names where known, e.g. void lbColl_80009DD4(Vec3* v0, Vec3* v1, GXColor* clr).
Unmatched structural work mostly retained argN/temp/sp-style names, which is appropriate when semantics are still uncertain and stack matching is the priority.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adjusted PAD_STACK values, including lbColl_80006094 from 64 to 56, lbColl_800067F8 from 16 to 24, and lbColl_80006E58 from 144 to 112.
Changed Vec3 storage strategy: whole-struct initializers like Vec3 vec4 = *arg0 and Vec3 c0 = *c were introduced, while some temporary vectors were scalarized into individual floats.
Reordered local declarations, including lbColl_80009DD4's float locals and lbColl_8000A584's Mtx sp40 placement, to influence stack offsets.
Replaced approximatelyZero/between-style abstractions with nearzero using volatile loads from named constants, aligning branch and constant-load behavior.
Parenthesized vector projection formulas so division applies to the full dot-product numerator/denominator, matching intended assembly order rather than C precedence surprises.
Restored actual HSD_MtxInverse and PSMTXMultVec calls in lbColl_80006E58 using a local Mtx sp9C where the old code had commented-out matrix operations.
Updated many PSMTXConcat/HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr call sites to use lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr so the same inline matrix setup pattern is emitted consistently.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the dump.
The decomp.dev bot reported overall matched code at 67.14%, +0.03%, +1016 bytes for GALE01.
The bot confirmed 2 new matches: lbColl_80005C44 and lbColl_80009DD4.
The bot confirmed 7 improvements in unmatched items, led by lbColl_8000A78C, lbColl_80006E58, lbColl_80006094, and lbColl_800067F8.
The PR body states verification was python configure.py && ninja plus pre-commit checks during commit.
follow_up_queries: Continue lbColl_80006094 stack/FPR drift investigation using the Vec3 copy and PAD_STACK tactics from this PR.
Continue lbColl_800067F8 structural drift investigation, especially nearzero calls and projection formula parenthesization.
Finish lbColl_80006E58 after the output-parameter fix; PR body says it still has structural stack/FPR drift.
Resolve lbColl_800077A0 sqrt spill/register difference noted in the PR body.
Investigate shared by-value Vec3 temporary layout mismatch in lbColl_8000A244, lbColl_8000A584, lbColl_8000A78C, lbColl_8000A95C, and lbColl_8000AB2C.
Audit future lbcollision call sites for lbColl_80006E58 argument order: Vec3 output, scale floats, then float* distance output.
